query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "swe-explore: benchmarking how coding agents explore repositories" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on the software development processes and organizational challenges of civic tech grassroots groups, which is a distinct construct from the user's question about how coding agents (LLMs) algorithmically explore code repositories. It fails to measure the same mechanism, variables, or empirical population (autonomous agents vs. human volunteers) and does not fall into any of the acceptance categories (a-f).
